entry.title: From Concepts to a Collaboration Plan: Developing SCEC’s 2026 Technical Activity Group (TAG) Portfolio – Statewide California Earthquake Center
entry.summary: This content is password protected. To view it please enter your password below:

entry.title: A First Look at SCEC’s Emerging Technical Activity Group (TAG) Concepts – Statewide California Earthquake Center
entry.summary: By: Tran Huynh and Edric Pauk

entry.title: CFM Fault Association Service Upgrade Adds Interactive 3D Fault Views and a New Fault Model – Statewide California Earthquake Center
entry.summary: By: Tran Huynh , Edric Pauk , Scott Marshall , Andreas Plesch , John H. Shaw

entry.title: Community-Driven Earthquake Science: Progress in SCEC’s Earth Models Development – Statewide California Earthquake Center
entry.summary: By: Tran Huynh , Edric Pauk , Scott Marshall , Patricia Persaud

entry.title: Quakeworx Kick-Off Workshop: Advances Cybertraining and Democratizes Access in Computational Seismology – Statewide California Earthquake Center
entry.summary: By: Alice-Agnes Gabriel , Amit Chourasia , Philip Maechling , David A. May , Ahmed Elbanna , Yehuda Ben-Zion

entry.title: Researchers head to Greenland for ambitious science mission - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: An international team is collecting observations to understand how a melting Greenland could cause weather change in UK and Europe.

entry.title: World-class UK research organisations launch new group - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: The NRO launches today, bringing together more than 100 leaders from government, research, academia, industry and funding organisations.

entry.title: Modernisation at Rothera Research Station shortlisted for British Construction and Infrastructure Award - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: British Antarctic Survey’s (BAS) Modernisation Project at Rothera Research Station has been shortlisted in four categories at the British Construction and Infrastructure Awards (BCIA) 2026.

entry.title: Human activity has driven retreat of one of Antarctica's most important glaciers - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: British Antarctic Survey scientists have contributed to the first study to directly link the retreat of a major Antarctic glacier to human-caused climate change.

entry.title: Antarctica's first dinosaur fossil confirmed from 1985 Antarctic expedition - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: A bone collected nearly 40 years ago has been formally described as the first dinosaur fossil ever found on the Antarctic continent, discovered by Dr Mike Thomson, a BAS geologist, during a 1985 expedition.

entry.title: Think it’s hot here? The Antarctic Peninsula is unusually warm too - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: As extreme summer heat causes disruption across the UK and Europe this week, Midwinter at Antarctica’s Rothera Research Station is becoming increasingly unrecognisable.

entry.title: A long look back on Midwinter's Day - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: June 21 marks Midwinter's Day - the shortest day of the year in Antarctica, and a celebrated tradition throughout our history.

entry.title: Extreme warm weather is changing Antarctic science - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: Antarctica has been experiencing more extreme warm weather. These changes affect global climate systems, and make it harder to carry out our science.

entry.title: Rothera Research Station's construction season upgrades safeguard future polar science - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: A significant phase of construction at Rothera has been completed this season, delivering new infrastructure that will power cutting-edge polar science for decades ahead.

entry.title: New study provides clues to predicting iceberg calving - British Antarctic Survey
entry.summary: New research led by British Antarctic Survey provides fresh insights into how cracks and fractures develop in ice and how future sea-level rise may unfold.
